description_long: This is a compact, vintage-style military airplane with a predominantly olive green fuselage and wings, accented by red stars and markings, suggesting Soviet-era origins. The aircraft features a single radial engine at the nose, with a prominent propeller and a rounded engine cowling. Its wings are straight and mounted at mid-fuselage, with no visible sweep or winglets, and the undercarriage consists of two main wheels and a small tailwheel, all fixed and exposed. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is positioned forward on the fuselage, enclosed with a small canopy, and appears to be designed for a single pilot. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for short takeoff and landing operations in urban or rugged environments.
description: A compact, olive green vintage military airplane with red star markings, a single radial engine, and fixed landing gear. It features a forward, enclosed cockpit and a conventional tail design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the olive green fuselage, red star markings, and the single radial engine with propeller at the nose.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the round engine cowling, propeller, and the symmetrical straight wings with red stars.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail number, red star, and the fixed landing gear beneath the wings.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and the fixed main and tail wheels.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight, unswept wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a small canopy.
